final_response_sheet.title: Claim, Evidence, Action
final_response_sheet.prompt: Use one clear claim, one strong piece of evidence, and one realistic next step to explain your position.
planning_reminders: Keep each section short and specific.
Choose one piece of evidence that actually helps your claim.

render_hints.purpose_line: This final response works best as a short, structured explanation.
render_hints.response_note: One clear idea in each box is enough.
quick_check_items: My evidence actually supports the claim.

final_response_sheet.title: Cause and Effect Chain
final_response_sheet.prompt: Explain how one choice or event leads to the next part of the situation.
planning_reminders: Use your own words instead of copying the source.

render_hints.purpose_line: Show the sequence, not just the ending.
render_hints.response_note: Short notes work better than one long paragraph here.

success_criteria: I explained the middle step, not just the start and end.

final_response_sheet.title: Map the Decision
final_response_sheet.prompt: Record the strongest parts of the decision in a simple matrix so each part stays visible.

render_hints.purpose_line: Use the grid to keep the parts separated and readable.
render_hints.response_note: Short notes in the grid are enough.

success_criteria: I used the grid instead of turning everything into a paragraph.

final_response_sheet.title: Need, Role, Response
final_response_sheet.prompt: Show the need, the people affected, the role that helps, and why that response matters.
planning_reminders: Choose one real need or risk.
Make sure the response role actually fits the problem.

render_hints.purpose_line: This format works best when each part stays concrete.
render_hints.response_note: Keep each section specific and believable.

success_criteria: The response role actually matches the need.
